title: Ticketing & ITSM Integrations Overview
content:
## What is Ticketing & ITSM?

Ticketing and IT Service Management (ITSM) tools provide structured workflows for tracking, managing, and resolving incidents, service requests, changes, and problems across an organization. In security operations, ticketing systems serve as the official record of incident response activities, enabling accountability, audit trails, SLA tracking, and cross-team collaboration. ITSM platforms bridge the gap between security teams and IT operations, ensuring that security incidents are tracked through resolution with proper documentation and stakeholder communication.

## Why Integrate Ticketing with T1 Agentics?

Connecting your ticketing and ITSM platforms to T1 Agentics creates a seamless workflow from alert detection through incident resolution. This integration enables:

- **Automated Ticket Creation**: When T1 Agentics confirms a security incident, automatically create a ticket in your ITSM system with all relevant context, enrichment data, and recommended actions
- **Bidirectional Status Sync**: Keep incident statuses synchronized between T1 Agentics and your ticketing system so all stakeholders see the current state
- **SLA Tracking**: Leverage your ITSM platform's SLA engine to track security incident response times against organizational commitments
- **Escalation Management**: Automatically escalate tickets based on severity, time elapsed, or new findings discovered during T1 Agentics investigation
- **Post-Incident Documentation**: Generate comprehensive incident reports in your ticketing system with timeline, actions taken, and lessons learned
- **Cross-Team Collaboration**: Route security incidents to IT operations, application owners, or management through familiar ticketing workflows

## Available Integrations

T1 Agentics supports integrations with the following ticketing and ITSM platforms:

| Integration | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| **ServiceNow ITSM** | Enterprise ITSM platform with incident, problem, change management, and CMDB integration |
| **ServiceNow** | Core ServiceNow platform integration for custom table and workflow access |
| **Jira** | Atlassian's project and issue tracking platform widely used for security operations task management |
| **Jira Service Management** | Atlassian's ITSM solution with SLA management, queues, and customer portal |
| **PagerDuty** | Digital operations management with on-call scheduling, escalation policies, and incident response |
| **OpsGenie** | Alert management and on-call scheduling with multi-channel notifications and escalation rules |
| **Zendesk** | Customer service and ticketing platform with workflow automation and SLA management |
| **BMC Helix** | AI-powered ITSM with predictive service management and multi-cloud operations |
| **BMC Remedy** | Enterprise ITSM with ITIL-aligned workflows and comprehensive asset management |
| **ConnectWise** | MSP-focused PSA and ITSM platform with ticketing, billing, and project management |
| **Freshservice** | Cloud-based ITSM with AI-powered service desk, asset management, and workflow automation |
| **Fresh Service** | Additional Freshservice integration path for legacy API connectivity |
| **FireHydrant** | Incident management platform with automated runbooks, status pages, and retrospectives |
| **Incident.io** | Modern incident management with Slack-native workflows, status pages, and post-incident learning |
| **ManageEngine ServiceDesk** | ITSM with integrated asset management, CMDB, and project management |
| **ServiceDeskPlus** | ManageEngine's comprehensive IT help desk with multi-site support |
| **Rootly** | Incident management platform with automated workflows, on-call management, and retrospectives |
| **RSA Archer** | Integrated risk management platform with incident management and compliance workflows |
| **Splunk On-Call (VictorOps)** | Incident response and on-call management with real-time collaboration and alert routing |
| **Squadcast** | Incident management with SRE workflows, runbooks, and automated remediation |
| **xMatters** | Service reliability platform with adaptive incident management and on-call scheduling |
| **TheHive** | Open-source security incident response platform with case management and observable analysis |
| **Ivanti ITSM** | Enterprise service management with self-service automation and voice-enabled workflows |
| **Azure DevOps** | Microsoft's development platform with work item tracking for security-related tasks |
| **Redmine** | Open-source project management and issue tracking with customizable workflows |
| **Request Tracker** | Open-source ticketing system widely used in academic and government environments |
| **Remedyforce** | Salesforce-based ITSM with cloud service management and self-service portal |
| **Cherwell** | Service management platform with codeless configuration and process automation |
| **Resilient** | IBM's security incident response platform with dynamic playbooks and privacy breach response |
| **StatusPage** | Atlassian's status page service for communicating incidents to customers and stakeholders |
| **Salesforce** | CRM platform integration for security incidents affecting customer-facing services |
| **Google Vault** | Google Workspace eDiscovery and retention tool for preserving incident-related data |
| **FireEye Central Management** | Centralized management console for FireEye security products |
| **PhishLabs** | Digital risk protection with managed phishing takedown services |
| **Radar** | Privacy incident management with breach notification assessment and regulatory compliance |

## Key Capabilities

When you connect ticketing tools to T1 Agentics, the following capabilities become available:

- **Ticket Creation**: Automatically create incidents, service requests, or work items in your ITSM when security alerts are confirmed
- **Ticket Updates**: Push investigation findings, enrichment data, and status changes to existing tickets
- **Bidirectional Sync**: Synchronize ticket status, priority, assignee, and notes between T1 Agentics and your ITSM
- **On-Call Notification**: Trigger PagerDuty, OpsGenie, or xMatters alerts to notify the right responder based on escalation policies
- **SLA Monitoring**: Track response and resolution SLAs for security incidents through your ITSM's SLA engine
- **Attachment Support**: Attach forensic artifacts, screenshots, and investigation reports to tickets
- **Custom Field Mapping**: Map T1 Agentics incident fields to custom ITSM fields for consistent data capture
- **Bulk Operations**: Manage multiple related tickets when a single security incident affects multiple systems or teams

## Getting Started

To connect a ticketing integration in T1 Agentics:

1. Navigate to **Connect** from the left sidebar
2. Click **Marketplace** to open the integration catalog
3. Use the category filter to select **Ticketing** or search by vendor name
4. Click **Configure** on your desired ticketing integration
5. Enter the required connection details (instance URL, API tokens, service accounts) as specified in the individual integration guide
6. Click **Test Connection** to verify connectivity
7. Configure field mapping and default values for ticket creation

## Best Practices

- **Map fields carefully**: Spend time mapping T1 Agentics incident fields to your ITSM fields. Good field mapping ensures tickets contain actionable information
- **Use templates**: Create ITSM ticket templates for different incident types (malware, phishing, data breach) with pre-populated fields
- **Configure bidirectional sync**: Ensure status changes in either system are reflected in both to avoid conflicting information
- **Set appropriate default priorities**: Map T1 Agentics severity levels to your ITSM priority levels consistently
- **Include enrichment data**: Configure playbooks to include threat intelligence enrichment, affected asset details, and recommended actions in created tickets
- **Establish escalation paths**: Define clear escalation rules for when tickets should be escalated based on severity or elapsed time
- **Test ticket creation thoroughly**: Validate that tickets are created with correct fields, assignments, and formatting before enabling automated creation
- **Maintain audit trails**: Use ticket comments to log all investigation steps and response actions for compliance and post-incident review

## Related Playbooks

- **Security Incident Ticket Creation**: Automated ticket creation with full incident context, enrichment data, and MITRE ATT&CK mapping
- **Escalation Workflow**: Time-based and severity-based escalation through PagerDuty, OpsGenie, or native ITSM escalation policies
- **Incident Status Synchronization**: Bidirectional status and priority sync between T1 Agentics and your ITSM platform
- **Post-Incident Report Generation**: Automated creation of comprehensive incident reports attached to closure tickets
- **Major Incident Coordination**: Multi-team coordination workflow with status page updates, stakeholder notifications, and bridge call management
- **Change Request Automation**: Create change requests for remediation actions that require change advisory board approval
- **SLA Breach Alert**: Automated escalation and notification when security incident SLAs are approaching breach thresholds
